---
title: "NY HP Rates: Data Quality Checks"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

This notebook checks the quality of gas bill data in the NY HP rates master tables.
It compares gas bills before (upgrade 0) and after (upgrade 2) heat pump installation,
broken down by gas tariff transition, to verify that the gas calc fix
(`ny_20260307_r1-8_gascalcfix` batch) is producing sensible results.

## Setup

```{python}
from __future__ import annotations

import io
import math
from pathlib import Path
from typing import cast

import polars as pl
from lib.rdp import fetch_rdp_file
from lib.plotnine import SB_COLORS, theme_switchbox
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    annotate,
    coord_cartesian,
    element_text,
    facet_wrap,
    geom_col,
    geom_text,
    geom_vline,
    ggplot,
    guides,
    labs,
    scale_color_identity,
    scale_fill_manual,
    scale_x_continuous,
    scale_y_continuous,
    theme,
)
```

## Parameters

```{python}
BATCH = "ny_20260307_r1-8_gascalcfix"
STATE = "NY"
S3_BASE = "s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/outputs/hp_rates"

PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12 = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_1+2/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_34 = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_3+4/comb_bills_year_target/"

RDP_REF = "d0353e1"
UTILITIES = ["cenhud", "coned", "nimo", "nyseg", "or", "psegli", "rge"]
TARIFF_MAP_DIR = "rate_design/hp_rates/ny/config/tariff_maps/gas"

PATH_RDP_ROOT = Path("~/repos/rate-design-platform").expanduser()
PATH_LOAD_CURVES = PATH_RDP_ROOT / "data/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2/load_curve_monthly/state=NY"
PATH_METADATA_SB = "s3://data.sb/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2_sb/metadata_utility/state=NY/utility_assignment.parquet"
GAS_COL = "out.natural_gas.total.energy_consumption"

BLDG_ID = "bldg_id"
ANNUAL_MONTH = "Annual"
COOLING_COL = "in.hvac_cooling_partial_space_conditioning"
COOLING_GROUPS = ["Full cooling", "Partial cooling", "No cooling"]
UTIL_NAMES = {
    "cenhud": "Central Hudson",
    "coned": "Con Edison",
    "nimo": "National Grid",
    "nyseg": "NYSEG",
    "or": "O&R",
    "psegli": "PSEG-LI",
    "rge": "RG&E",
}
```

## Median bill, cost-of-service, and bill alignment by customer group

A high-level sanity check: for each customer group in the baseline scenario
(run 1+2), what is the weighted median annual bill, cost-of-service
(marginal cost + residual), and bill alignment (BAT)?

Cost-of-service = `economic_burden` + `residual_share` from the BAT table.
BAT = bill − cost-of-service. A positive BAT means the customer overpays
relative to their cost of service; a negative BAT means they underpay.

```{python}
PATH_BAT_12 = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_1+2/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"

bat = pl.read_parquet(PATH_BAT_12, hive_partitioning=True).with_columns(
    (pl.col("economic_burden_delivery") + pl.col("residual_share_delivery")).alias("cos_delivery"),
    (pl.col("economic_burden_total") + pl.col("residual_share_total")).alias("cos_total"),
)
print(f"BAT table: {bat.height} rows")
```

```{python}
def _weighted_median(df: pl.DataFrame, col: str, w_col: str = "weight") -> float:
    s = df.sort(col)
    cum = s[w_col].cum_sum() / s[w_col].sum()
    return float(s.filter(cum >= 0.5)[col][0])


GROUPS = {
    "HP customers": bat.filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp")),
    "Non-HP customers": bat.filter(~pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp")),
    "Fossil fuel customers": bat.filter(
        pl.col("heats_with_natgas") | pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane")
    ),
    "Natural gas customers": bat.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas")),
    "Oil/propane customers": bat.filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane")),
    "Electric resistance customers": bat.filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type") == "electrical_resistance"),
}

summary_rows = []
for name, g in GROUPS.items():
    def _wmean(col: str) -> float:
        return (g[col] * g["weight"]).sum() / g["weight"].sum()

    summary_rows.append({
        "Group": name,
        "N (weighted)": round(g["weight"].sum()),
        "Med. Bill (delivery)": round(_weighted_median(g, "annual_bill_delivery")),
        "Med. CoS (delivery)": round(_weighted_median(g, "cos_delivery")),
        "Med. BAT (delivery)": round(_weighted_median(g, "BAT_percustomer_delivery")),
        "Mean BAT (delivery)": round(_wmean("BAT_percustomer_delivery")),
        "Med. Bill (total)": round(_weighted_median(g, "annual_bill_total")),
        "Med. CoS (total)": round(_weighted_median(g, "cos_total")),
        "Med. BAT (total)": round(_weighted_median(g, "BAT_percustomer_total")),
        "Mean BAT (total)": round(_wmean("BAT_percustomer_total")),
    })

summary = pl.DataFrame(summary_rows)
summary
```

HP customers overpay (positive BAT) because current volumetric delivery rates
charge them more than their marginal cost of service. Non-HP and natural gas
customers underpay (negative BAT) — they are cross-subsidized under current
rates.

### Same table under HP seasonal rate (run 5+6)

Same groups and columns, but now under the HP seasonal rate scenario where HP
customers get cost-reflective delivery rates and non-HP customers stay on the
default flat rate.

```{python}
PATH_BAT_56 = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_5+6/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"

bat_56 = pl.read_parquet(PATH_BAT_56, hive_partitioning=True).with_columns(
    (pl.col("economic_burden_delivery") + pl.col("residual_share_delivery")).alias("cos_delivery"),
    (pl.col("economic_burden_total") + pl.col("residual_share_total")).alias("cos_total"),
)
print(f"BAT table (run 5+6): {bat_56.height} rows")
```

```{python}
GROUPS_56 = {
    "HP customers": bat_56.filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp")),
    "Non-HP customers": bat_56.filter(~pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp")),
    "Fossil fuel customers": bat_56.filter(
        pl.col("heats_with_natgas") | pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane")
    ),
    "Natural gas customers": bat_56.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas")),
    "Oil/propane customers": bat_56.filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane")),
    "Electric resistance customers": bat_56.filter(
        pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type") == "electrical_resistance"
    ),
}

summary_rows_56 = []
for name, g in GROUPS_56.items():
    def _wmean(col: str) -> float:
        return (g[col] * g["weight"]).sum() / g["weight"].sum()

    summary_rows_56.append({
        "Group": name,
        "N (weighted)": round(g["weight"].sum()),
        "Med. Bill (delivery)": round(_weighted_median(g, "annual_bill_delivery")),
        "Med. CoS (delivery)": round(_weighted_median(g, "cos_delivery")),
        "Med. BAT (delivery)": round(_weighted_median(g, "BAT_percustomer_delivery")),
        "Mean BAT (delivery)": round(_wmean("BAT_percustomer_delivery")),
        "Med. Bill (total)": round(_weighted_median(g, "annual_bill_total")),
        "Med. CoS (total)": round(_weighted_median(g, "cos_total")),
        "Med. BAT (total)": round(_weighted_median(g, "BAT_percustomer_total")),
        "Mean BAT (total)": round(_wmean("BAT_percustomer_total")),
    })

summary_56 = pl.DataFrame(summary_rows_56)
summary_56
```

## Load data

We load the master bills for both upgrade scenarios (annual rows only) and the gas tariff
maps that tell us which gas tariff each building is assigned to.

```{python}
bills_u0 = pl.read_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12).filter(pl.col("month") == "Annual")
bills_u2 = pl.read_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_34).filter(pl.col("month") == "Annual")
print(f"u0: {bills_u0.height} buildings, u2: {bills_u2.height} buildings")
```

```{python}
u00_frames = []
u02_frames = []
for util in UTILITIES:
    for suffix, dest in [("_u00.csv", u00_frames), ("_u02.csv", u02_frames)]:
        csv_text = fetch_rdp_file(f"{TARIFF_MAP_DIR}/{util}{suffix}", ref=RDP_REF)
        dest.append(pl.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_text)))

tariff_u0 = pl.concat(u00_frames).rename({"tariff_key": "tariff_u0"})
tariff_u2 = pl.concat(u02_frames).rename({"tariff_key": "tariff_u2"})
print(f"Tariff maps: u0={tariff_u0.height} rows, u2={tariff_u2.height} rows")
```

We also load the monthly ResStock load curves (local) to compute annual gas consumption per
building for each upgrade, so we can show the median consumption decline alongside the bill
change.

```{python}
gas_u0 = (
    pl.scan_parquet(f"{PATH_LOAD_CURVES}/upgrade=00/")
    .select("bldg_id", GAS_COL)
    .group_by("bldg_id")
    .agg(pl.col(GAS_COL).sum().alias("gas_kwh_u0"))
    .collect()
)
gas_u2 = (
    pl.scan_parquet(f"{PATH_LOAD_CURVES}/upgrade=02/")
    .select("bldg_id", GAS_COL)
    .group_by("bldg_id")
    .agg(pl.col(GAS_COL).sum().alias("gas_kwh_u2"))
    .collect()
)
print(f"Gas consumption: u0={gas_u0.height} buildings, u2={gas_u2.height} buildings")
```

## Build comparison table

We join the two upgrade scenarios on `bldg_id`, restrict to natgas-heated buildings with
nonzero gas bills in u0, and attach the gas tariff keys from both upgrade maps.

```{python}
merged = (
    bills_u0.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("bldg_id", "sb.electric_utility", pl.col("gas_total_bill").alias("gas_u0"))
    .join(
        bills_u2.select("bldg_id", pl.col("gas_total_bill").alias("gas_u2")),
        on="bldg_id",
    )
    .filter(pl.col("gas_u0") > 0)
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("gas_u2") - pl.col("gas_u0")).alias("gas_diff"),
        ((pl.col("gas_u2") - pl.col("gas_u0")) / pl.col("gas_u0") * 100).alias("gas_pct"),
    )
    .join(tariff_u0, on="bldg_id", how="left")
    .join(tariff_u2, on="bldg_id", how="left")
    .join(gas_u0, on="bldg_id", how="left")
    .join(gas_u2, on="bldg_id", how="left")
    .with_columns(
        ((pl.col("gas_kwh_u2") - pl.col("gas_kwh_u0")) / pl.col("gas_kwh_u0") * 100)
        .alias("cons_pct"),
    )
)
print(f"{merged.height} natgas-heated buildings with nonzero gas in u0")
```

## Gas bill change by tariff transition

Each row shows how a group of buildings — defined by their gas tariff in u0 and u2 —
fares after the heat pump upgrade. We expect gas bills to decrease for most transitions
since gas consumption drops ~89% after switching to a heat pump.

```{python}
transitions = (
    merged
    .group_by("sb.electric_utility", "tariff_u0", "tariff_u2")
    .agg(
        pl.len().alias("n"),
        pl.col("gas_u0").median().alias("median_gas_u0"),
        pl.col("gas_u2").median().alias("median_gas_u2"),
        pl.col("gas_diff").median().alias("median_diff"),
        pl.col("gas_pct").median().alias("median_pct"),
        pl.col("cons_pct").median().alias("median_cons_pct"),
        (pl.col("gas_diff") > 0).sum().alias("n_increase"),
        (pl.col("gas_diff") < 0).sum().alias("n_decrease"),
    )
    .sort("median_pct", descending=True)
)

header = (
    f"{'electric_utility':>16s}  {'gas tariff in u0':>30s} → {'u2':<30s}  "
    f"{'n':>5s}  {'med_u0':>8s}  {'med_u2':>8s}  {'med_chg':>8s}  "
    f"{'med_%':>7s}  {'med_cons%':>9s}  {'#up':>5s}  {'#down':>5s}"
)
print(header)
print("-" * len(header))
for row in transitions.iter_rows(named=True):
    print(
        f"{row['sb.electric_utility']:>16s}  "
        f"{row['tariff_u0']:>30s} → {row['tariff_u2']:<30s}  "
        f"{row['n']:>5d}  "
        f"${row['median_gas_u0']:>7.2f}  "
        f"${row['median_gas_u2']:>7.2f}  "
        f"${row['median_diff']:>+7.2f}  "
        f"{row['median_pct']:>+6.1f}%  "
        f"{row['median_cons_pct']:>+8.1f}%  "
        f"{row['n_increase']:>5d}  "
        f"{row['n_decrease']:>5d}"
    )
```

## Summary by utility

```{python}
by_util = (
    merged
    .group_by("sb.electric_utility")
    .agg(
        pl.len().alias("n"),
        pl.col("gas_u0").median().alias("median_gas_u0"),
        pl.col("gas_u2").median().alias("median_gas_u2"),
        pl.col("gas_diff").median().alias("median_diff"),
        pl.col("gas_pct").median().alias("median_pct"),
        pl.col("cons_pct").median().alias("median_cons_pct"),
        (pl.col("gas_diff") > 0).sum().alias("n_increase"),
        (pl.col("gas_diff") < 0).sum().alias("n_decrease"),
    )
    .sort("sb.electric_utility")
)

header = (
    f"{'electric_utility':>16s}  {'n':>5s}  {'med_u0':>8s}  {'med_u2':>8s}  "
    f"{'med_chg':>8s}  {'med_%':>7s}  {'med_cons%':>9s}  {'#up':>5s}  {'#down':>5s}"
)
print(header)
print("-" * len(header))
for row in by_util.iter_rows(named=True):
    print(
        f"{row['sb.electric_utility']:>16s}  "
        f"{row['n']:>5d}  "
        f"${row['median_gas_u0']:>7.2f}  "
        f"${row['median_gas_u2']:>7.2f}  "
        f"${row['median_diff']:>+7.2f}  "
        f"{row['median_pct']:>+6.1f}%  "
        f"{row['median_cons_pct']:>+8.1f}%  "
        f"{row['n_increase']:>5d}  "
        f"{row['n_decrease']:>5d}"
    )

total = merged.height
total_inc = merged.filter(pl.col("gas_diff") > 0).height
total_dec = merged.filter(pl.col("gas_diff") < 0).height
print(
    f"\n  STATEWIDE: {total} natgas bldgs, "
    f"{total_inc} increase ({total_inc / total * 100:.1f}%), "
    f"{total_dec} decrease ({total_dec / total * 100:.1f}%)"
)
print(
    f"  Median: u0=${merged['gas_u0'].median():,.2f}  "
    f"u2=${merged['gas_u2'].median():,.2f}  "
    f"change=${merged['gas_diff'].median():+,.2f} "
    f"({merged['gas_pct'].median():+.1f}%)"
)
```

## Consumption vs. bill change scatterplot

Each point is a gas tariff transition group. The diagonal line shows where points would
fall if bills were purely volumetric (no fixed charge) and the volumetric rate didn't
change between u0 and u2. The gap between the data and that line is the effect of fixed
charges absorbing most of the consumption decline.

```{python}
#| label: fig-cons-vs-bill
#| fig-cap: "Median gas consumption change vs. median gas bill change by tariff transition"
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    annotate,
    coord_cartesian,
    geom_abline,
    geom_hline,
    geom_point,
    geom_text,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    scale_x_reverse,
    scale_y_reverse,
    theme,
    theme_minimal,
)

scatter_df = (
    merged
    .group_by("sb.electric_utility", "tariff_u0", "tariff_u2")
    .agg(
        pl.len().alias("n"),
        pl.col("gas_pct").median().alias("median_bill_pct"),
        pl.col("cons_pct").median().alias("median_cons_pct"),
    )
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("sb.electric_utility") + ": " + pl.col("tariff_u0") + " -> " + pl.col("tariff_u2"))
        .alias("label"),
    )
    .to_pandas()
)

label_df = scatter_df[scatter_df["median_bill_pct"] > 0].copy()
label_df = label_df.sort_values("median_bill_pct", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
label_df["label_y"] = -0.5 - label_df.index * 2.0

(
    ggplot(scatter_df, aes(x="median_cons_pct", y="median_bill_pct", size="n"))
    + geom_abline(intercept=0, slope=1, color="#023047", alpha=0.35, size=0.8)
    + annotate(
        "text", x=-15, y=-17,
        label="Purely volumetric,\nsame rate",
        size=7, color="#023047", alpha=0.6, ha="right", va="bottom",
    )
    + geom_hline(yintercept=0, color="grey", alpha=0.4, linetype="dotted")
    + geom_point(aes(color="sb.electric_utility"), alpha=0.7)
    + geom_text(
        label_df,
        aes(x="median_cons_pct", y="label_y", label="label"),
        size=7,
        va="top",
        inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + labs(
        x="Median gas consumption change (%)",
        y="Median gas bill change (%)",
        size="Buildings",
        color="Electric utility",
    )
    + scale_x_reverse()
    + scale_y_reverse()
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(0, -100), ylim=(15, -75))
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(10, 10))
)
```

## Building-level consumption vs. bill change

Same axes as above, but each point is an individual building rather than a tariff-transition
group, faceted by electric utility and colored by gas tariff transition. Low opacity reveals
where buildings cluster — the horizontal banding within each panel shows how different gas
tariff structures compress bill changes relative to consumption changes.

```{python}
#| label: fig-bldg-cons-vs-bill
#| fig-cap: "Gas consumption change vs. gas bill change per building, faceted by utility and colored by gas tariff transition"
import pandas as pd

from plotnine import (
    coord_cartesian,
    facet_wrap,
    geom_text,
)

bldg_scatter = (
    merged
    .filter(pl.col("gas_kwh_u0") > 0)
    .select("cons_pct", "gas_pct", "sb.electric_utility", "tariff_u0", "tariff_u2")
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("tariff_u0") + " -> " + pl.col("tariff_u2")).alias("gas_transition"),
    )
    .rename({"gas_pct": "bill_pct"})
    .to_pandas()
)

y_min = bldg_scatter["bill_pct"].min()
legend_start = y_min - 5

legend_rows = []
for util, grp in bldg_scatter.groupby("sb.electric_utility"):
    transitions = sorted(grp["gas_transition"].unique())
    for i, t in enumerate(transitions):
        legend_rows.append({
            "sb.electric_utility": util,
            "gas_transition": t,
            "cons_pct": -1,
            "bill_pct": legend_start - i * 5,
        })
legend_df = pd.DataFrame(legend_rows)

n_utils = bldg_scatter["sb.electric_utility"].nunique()

(
    ggplot(bldg_scatter, aes(x="cons_pct", y="bill_pct", color="gas_transition"))
    + geom_abline(intercept=0, slope=1, color="#023047", alpha=0.25, size=0.6)
    + geom_hline(yintercept=0, color="grey", alpha=0.4, linetype="dotted")
    + geom_point(alpha=0.25, size=2.0, stroke=0)
    + geom_text(
        legend_df,
        aes(x="cons_pct", y="bill_pct", label="gas_transition", color="gas_transition"),
        size=9, ha="left", nudge_x=-1,
        inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + labs(
        x="Gas consumption change (%)",
        y="Gas bill change (%)",
    )
    + facet_wrap("sb.electric_utility", ncol=1)
    + scale_x_reverse()
    + scale_y_reverse()
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(10, min(18, 3.5 * n_utils)), legend_position="none")
)
```

## Gas bill: fixed charge share

For each gas-heated building in u0, what fraction of its annual gas bill is the
fixed charge? The fixed charge comes from the URDB gas tariff JSON
(`fixedchargefirstmeter` × 12 months); the annual gas bill comes from the
master bills table.

```{python}
import json

GAS_TARIFF_DIR = "rate_design/hp_rates/ny/config/tariffs/gas"

gas_tariff_keys = tariff_u0["tariff_u0"].unique().to_list()

fixed_rows: list[dict] = []
for key in gas_tariff_keys:
    if key == "null_gas_tariff":
        fixed_rows.append({"tariff_u0": key, "monthly_fixed": 0.0})
        continue
    content = fetch_rdp_file(f"{GAS_TARIFF_DIR}/{key}.json", ref=RDP_REF)
    tariff = json.loads(content)
    item = tariff["items"][0] if "items" in tariff else tariff
    fixed_rows.append({
        "tariff_u0": key,
        "monthly_fixed": item.get("fixedchargefirstmeter", 0.0),
    })

gas_fixed = pl.DataFrame(fixed_rows).with_columns(
    (pl.col("monthly_fixed") * 12).alias("annual_fixed"),
)
print(gas_fixed.sort("tariff_u0"))
```

```{python}
gas_with_fixed = (
    merged
    .join(gas_fixed, on="tariff_u0", how="left")
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("annual_fixed") / pl.col("gas_u0") * 100).alias("fixed_pct"),
    )
    .filter(pl.col("fixed_pct").is_finite())
)

print(f"{gas_with_fixed.height} buildings with computable fixed %")
print(f"Median fixed %: {gas_with_fixed['fixed_pct'].median():.1f}%")
print(f"Mean fixed %: {gas_with_fixed['fixed_pct'].mean():.1f}%")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-gas-fixed-pct
#| fig-cap: "Fraction of annual gas bill from fixed charges, by utility"
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    coord_cartesian,
    element_text,
    facet_wrap,
    geom_histogram,
    geom_text,
    geom_vline,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    scale_x_continuous,
    theme,
    theme_minimal,
)

gas_fixed_pd = gas_with_fixed.select(
    "fixed_pct", "sb.electric_utility",
).to_pandas()

medians_by_util = (
    gas_with_fixed
    .group_by("sb.electric_utility")
    .agg(pl.col("fixed_pct").median().alias("med_fixed"))
)

annot_pd = medians_by_util.with_columns(
    (
        pl.lit("median: ")
        + pl.col("med_fixed").round(0).cast(pl.Int32).cast(pl.Utf8)
        + pl.lit("%")
    ).alias("label"),
).to_pandas()

(
    ggplot(gas_fixed_pd, aes(x="fixed_pct"))
    + geom_histogram(binwidth=2, fill="#68bed8", color="white", alpha=0.85)
    + geom_vline(
        data=medians_by_util.to_pandas(),
        mapping=aes(xintercept="med_fixed"),
        linetype="dashed", color="#023047", size=0.7,
    )
    + geom_text(
        annot_pd,
        aes(x="med_fixed", y=float("inf"), label="label"),
        va="top", ha="left", nudge_x=1, size=8, color="#023047",
        inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + facet_wrap("sb.electric_utility", ncol=2, scales="free_y")
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=range(0, 101, 20))
    + labs(
        x="Fixed charge as % of annual gas bill",
        y="# Buildings",
        title="Gas bill fixed charge share — natgas-heated buildings (u0)",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(10, 10),
        strip_text=element_text(size=10, fontweight="bold"),
    )
)
```

## Annual gas bill distribution

How much do gas-heated buildings actually pay per year in gas bills? Faceted by
electric utility, with median annotated.

```{python}
#| label: fig-gas-bill-distribution
#| fig-cap: "Annual gas bills for natgas-heated buildings (u0), by utility"
gas_bills_pd = merged.select(
    pl.col("gas_u0").alias("annual_gas_bill"),
    "sb.electric_utility",
).to_pandas()

med_bills = (
    merged.group_by("sb.electric_utility")
    .agg(pl.col("gas_u0").median().alias("med_bill"))
)

med_labels = med_bills.with_columns(
    (pl.lit("median: $") + pl.col("med_bill").round(0).cast(pl.Int32).cast(pl.Utf8)).alias("label"),
).to_pandas()

(
    ggplot(gas_bills_pd, aes(x="annual_gas_bill"))
    + geom_histogram(binwidth=50, fill="#68bed8", color="white", alpha=0.85)
    + geom_vline(
        data=med_bills.to_pandas(),
        mapping=aes(xintercept="med_bill"),
        linetype="dashed", color="#023047", size=0.7,
    )
    + geom_text(
        med_labels,
        aes(x="med_bill", y=float("inf"), label="label"),
        va="top", ha="left", nudge_x=20, size=8, color="#023047",
        inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + facet_wrap("sb.electric_utility", ncol=2, scales="free_y")
    + labs(
        x="Annual Gas Bill ($)",
        y="# Buildings",
        title="Annual gas bill distribution — natgas-heated buildings (u0)",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(10, 10),
        strip_text=element_text(size=10, fontweight="bold"),
    )
)
```

## Bill change by cooling type — natgas-heated households

How does the annual bill change distribution for natural gas-heated households
differ by baseline cooling coverage? Homes with no cooling gain air conditioning
from the heat pump (gain of function), which increases their post-HP electricity
consumption and offsets some of the gas savings.

```{python}
#| label: load-metadata-and-deltas

metadata_sb = pl.read_parquet(PATH_METADATA_SB)

delta_state = (
    bills_u0.filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(BLDG_ID, "weight", pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_before"), "heats_with_natgas")
    .join(
        bills_u2.filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after")),
        on=BLDG_ID,
    )
    .with_columns((pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta"))
)
```

```{python}
#| label: histogram-helpers

BIN_WIDTH = 100
QUADRANT_COLORS = {
    "savings > $1k": "#1b5e20",
    "savings $0-1k": "#81c784",
    "losses $0-1k": "#ef9a9a",
    "losses > $1k": "#b71c1c",
}
QUADRANT_ORDER = list(QUADRANT_COLORS.keys())


def _weighted_percentile(df: pl.DataFrame, col: str, w_col: str, q: float) -> float:
    """Weighted percentile via cumulative weight."""
    s = df.sort(col)
    cum = s[w_col].cum_sum() / s[w_col].sum()
    return float(s.filter(cum >= q)[col][0])


def quadrant_pcts(df: pl.DataFrame) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Weighted percentage of homes in each bill-change quadrant."""
    total = df["weight"].sum()
    return {
        "savings > $1k": df.filter(pl.col("delta") < -1000)["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
        "savings $0-1k": df.filter((pl.col("delta") >= -1000) & (pl.col("delta") < 0))["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
        "losses $0-1k": df.filter((pl.col("delta") >= 0) & (pl.col("delta") < 1000))["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
        "losses > $1k": df.filter(pl.col("delta") >= 1000)["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
    }


def plot_bill_change_histogram_faceted(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    facet_col: str,
    facet_order: list[str],
    bin_width: int = BIN_WIDTH,
) -> ggplot:
    """Weighted histogram of annual bill change, faceted by a categorical column."""
    snap = max(bin_width, 50)
    x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(df, "delta", "weight", 0.01) / snap) * snap
    x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(df, "delta", "weight", 0.99) / snap) * snap

    facet_dtype = pl.Enum(facet_order)
    quadrant_dtype = pl.Enum(QUADRANT_ORDER)

    binned = (
        df.with_columns(
            ((pl.col("delta") / bin_width).floor() * bin_width + bin_width / 2).alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") <= -1000)
            .then(pl.lit("savings > $1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") > -1000) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 0))
            .then(pl.lit("savings $0-1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") >= 0) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 1000))
            .then(pl.lit("losses $0-1k"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("losses > $1k"))
            .alias("quadrant"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "quadrant", facet_col)
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("quadrant").cast(quadrant_dtype),
            pl.col(facet_col).cast(facet_dtype),
        )
    )

    annot_rows: list[dict] = []
    quadrant_bounds = [(x_lo, -1000), (-1000, 0), (0, 1000), (1000, x_hi)]
    label_xs = [(max(lo, x_lo) + min(hi, x_hi)) / 2 for lo, hi in quadrant_bounds]

    for fv in facet_order:
        facet_df = df.filter(pl.col(facet_col) == fv)
        if facet_df.is_empty():
            continue
        pct = quadrant_pcts(facet_df)
        facet_binned = binned.filter(pl.col(facet_col) == fv)
        max_w = float(facet_binned["weight_sum"].max()) if not facet_binned.is_empty() else 0.0
        y_val = max_w * 1.08
        for label, x_pos in zip(QUADRANT_ORDER, label_xs):
            if pct[label] < 0.5:
                continue
            annot_rows.append({
                facet_col: fv,
                "x": x_pos,
                "y": y_val,
                "label_text": f"{label}\n{pct[label]:.0f}%",
                "color_hex": QUADRANT_COLORS[label],
            })

    annot_df = pl.DataFrame(annot_rows).with_columns(
        pl.col(facet_col).cast(facet_dtype),
    )

    p = (
        ggplot(binned, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="quadrant"))
        + geom_col(width=bin_width * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=[-1000, 0, 1000], linetype="dotted", color="gray")
        + scale_fill_manual(values=QUADRANT_COLORS, breaks=QUADRANT_ORDER)
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(x_lo, x_hi))
        + facet_wrap(facet_col, ncol=1, scales="free_y")
        + labs(x="Annual Bill Change ($)", y="# of Homes")
        + guides(fill=False)
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(
            figure_size=(10, 8),
            strip_text=element_text(size=11, weight="bold"),
        )
    )

    if not annot_df.is_empty():
        p = (
            p
            + geom_text(
                annot_df,
                aes(x="x", y="y", label="label_text", color="color_hex"),
                inherit_aes=False,
                ha="center", size=9,
            )
            + scale_color_identity()
        )

    return p
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-natgas-by-cooling
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution for natural gas-heated households by baseline cooling coverage (Statewide)"

cooling_lookup = metadata_sb.select(BLDG_ID, COOLING_COL)

natgas_by_cooling = (
    delta_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .join(cooling_lookup, on=BLDG_ID, how="left")
    .with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col(COOLING_COL) == "100% Conditioned").then(pl.lit("Full cooling"))
        .when(pl.col(COOLING_COL) == "None").then(pl.lit("No cooling"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("Partial cooling"))
        .alias("cooling_group")
    )
    .select("delta", "weight", "cooling_group")
)

plot_bill_change_histogram_faceted(natgas_by_cooling, "cooling_group", COOLING_GROUPS)
```

## Electricity consumption percent change by cooling type and utility

How much does a gas-heated home's electricity use change after switching to a
heat pump? The answer depends heavily on whether the home already had cooling.
Homes with no cooling gain air conditioning from the heat pump (gain of
function), inflating their summer electricity use on top of the heating
electrification. The histograms below show the percent change in annual
electricity consumption for gas-heated homes, grouped by baseline cooling
coverage and faceted by electric utility.

```{python}
#| label: compute-elec-pct-change

_cache_path = Path("../cache/annual_elec_kwh.parquet")

if _cache_path.exists():
    _annual_elec = pl.read_parquet(_cache_path)
else:
    _elec_col = "out.electricity.total.energy_consumption"
    _load_base = PATH_LOAD_CURVES

    _before = (
        pl.scan_parquet(str(_load_base / "upgrade=00" / "*.parquet"))
        .select("bldg_id", _elec_col)
        .group_by("bldg_id")
        .agg(pl.col(_elec_col).sum().alias("elec_kwh_before"))
        .collect()
    )
    _after = (
        pl.scan_parquet(str(_load_base / "upgrade=02" / "*.parquet"))
        .select("bldg_id", _elec_col)
        .group_by("bldg_id")
        .agg(pl.col(_elec_col).sum().alias("elec_kwh_after"))
        .collect()
    )
    _annual_elec = _before.join(_after, on="bldg_id", how="inner")
    Path("../cache").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    _annual_elec.write_parquet(_cache_path)

elec_pct_change = (
    _annual_elec
    .join(
        metadata_sb.select(BLDG_ID, "heats_with_natgas", "sb.electric_utility", COOLING_COL, "weight"),
        on=BLDG_ID,
        how="inner",
    )
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .filter(pl.col("elec_kwh_before").abs() > 10)
    .with_columns(
        ((pl.col("elec_kwh_after") - pl.col("elec_kwh_before")) / pl.col("elec_kwh_before") * 100)
        .alias("pct_change"),
        pl.when(pl.col(COOLING_COL) == "100% Conditioned").then(pl.lit("Full cooling"))
        .when(pl.col(COOLING_COL) == "None").then(pl.lit("No cooling"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("Partial cooling"))
        .alias("cooling_group"),
        pl.col("sb.electric_utility").replace_strict(UTIL_NAMES).alias("utility_label"),
    )
)

for cg in COOLING_GROUPS:
    _sub = elec_pct_change.filter(pl.col("cooling_group") == cg)
    _med = _weighted_percentile(_sub, "pct_change", "weight", 0.50)
    print(f"{cg}: n={len(_sub)}, weighted median pct change = {_med:.0f}%")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-elec-pct-change-full-cooling
#| fig-cap: "Percent change in annual electricity consumption after switching to a heat pump — gas-heated homes with full baseline cooling, by utility"

_ELEC_BIN = 10

def _plot_elec_pct_change(df: pl.DataFrame, title: str) -> ggplot:
    snap = max(_ELEC_BIN, 10)
    _lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(df, "pct_change", "weight", 0.01) / snap) * snap
    _hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(df, "pct_change", "weight", 0.99) / snap) * snap

    binned = (
        df.with_columns(
            ((pl.col("pct_change") / _ELEC_BIN).floor() * _ELEC_BIN + _ELEC_BIN / 2)
            .alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .group_by("utility_label", "bin_center")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") >= 0)
            .then(pl.lit("increase"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("decrease"))
            .alias("direction"),
        )
    )

    return (
        ggplot(binned, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="direction"))
        + geom_col(width=_ELEC_BIN * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="dotted", color="gray")
        + facet_wrap("utility_label", scales="free_y", ncol=4)
        + scale_fill_manual(values={"increase": SB_COLORS["carrot"], "decrease": SB_COLORS["sky"]})
        + scale_x_continuous(labels=lambda xs: [f"{x:,.0f}%" for x in xs])
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.1, 0))
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(_lo, _hi))
        + labs(x="Percent change in annual electricity consumption", y="Weighted households", title=title)
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(figure_size=(10, 5), legend_position="none")
    )

_plot_elec_pct_change(
    elec_pct_change.filter(pl.col("cooling_group") == "Full cooling"),
    "Full cooling — electricity change after switching to heat pump",
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-elec-pct-change-partial-cooling
#| fig-cap: "Percent change in annual electricity consumption after switching to a heat pump — gas-heated homes with partial baseline cooling, by utility"

_plot_elec_pct_change(
    elec_pct_change.filter(pl.col("cooling_group") == "Partial cooling"),
    "Partial cooling — electricity change after switching to heat pump",
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-elec-pct-change-no-cooling
#| fig-cap: "Percent change in annual electricity consumption after switching to a heat pump — gas-heated homes with no baseline cooling, by utility"

_plot_elec_pct_change(
    elec_pct_change.filter(pl.col("cooling_group") == "No cooling"),
    "No cooling — electricity change after switching to heat pump",
)
```

## Delivery bill percent change by cooling type and utility

The delivery bill (volumetric delivery + fixed charge) is the component most
directly affected by the cross-subsidy problem. How much does it increase after
switching to a heat pump? Homes with no baseline cooling see the largest
delivery bill increases because they gain air conditioning on top of heating
electrification.

```{python}
#| label: compute-delivery-pct-change

delivery_pct_change = (
    bills_u0
    .select(
        BLDG_ID,
        (pl.col("elec_delivery_bill") + pl.col("elec_fixed_charge")).alias("delivery_before"),
        "heats_with_natgas", "sb.electric_utility", "weight",
    )
    .join(
        bills_u2.select(
            BLDG_ID,
            (pl.col("elec_delivery_bill") + pl.col("elec_fixed_charge")).alias("delivery_after"),
        ),
        on=BLDG_ID,
    )
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .filter(pl.col("delivery_before").abs() > 1)
    .join(metadata_sb.select(BLDG_ID, COOLING_COL), on=BLDG_ID, how="left")
    .with_columns(
        ((pl.col("delivery_after") - pl.col("delivery_before")) / pl.col("delivery_before") * 100)
        .alias("pct_change"),
        pl.when(pl.col(COOLING_COL) == "100% Conditioned").then(pl.lit("Full cooling"))
        .when(pl.col(COOLING_COL) == "None").then(pl.lit("No cooling"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("Partial cooling"))
        .alias("cooling_group"),
        pl.col("sb.electric_utility").replace_strict(UTIL_NAMES).alias("utility_label"),
    )
)

for cg in COOLING_GROUPS:
    _sub = delivery_pct_change.filter(pl.col("cooling_group") == cg)
    _med = _weighted_percentile(_sub, "pct_change", "weight", 0.50)
    print(f"{cg}: n={len(_sub)}, weighted median delivery bill pct change = {_med:.0f}%")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-delivery-pct-change-full-cooling
#| fig-cap: "Percent change in annual delivery bill after switching to a heat pump — gas-heated homes with full baseline cooling, by utility"

_plot_elec_pct_change(
    delivery_pct_change.filter(pl.col("cooling_group") == "Full cooling"),
    "Full cooling — delivery bill change after switching to heat pump",
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-delivery-pct-change-partial-cooling
#| fig-cap: "Percent change in annual delivery bill after switching to a heat pump — gas-heated homes with partial baseline cooling, by utility"

_plot_elec_pct_change(
    delivery_pct_change.filter(pl.col("cooling_group") == "Partial cooling"),
    "Partial cooling — delivery bill change after switching to heat pump",
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-delivery-pct-change-no-cooling
#| fig-cap: "Percent change in annual delivery bill after switching to a heat pump — gas-heated homes with no baseline cooling, by utility"

_plot_elec_pct_change(
    delivery_pct_change.filter(pl.col("cooling_group") == "No cooling"),
    "No cooling — delivery bill change after switching to heat pump",
)
```

## Bill change histograms by fuel type (diagnostic)

Weighted histograms of annual bill change after switching from current HVAC to
heat pumps, under default rates. These were removed from the main analysis
notebook during streamlining and are preserved here for diagnostic reference.

```{python}
#| label: dq-bill-change-helpers

HEATING_TYPE_COL = "postprocess_group.heating_type"

HEATING_TYPE_LABELS: dict[str, str] = {
    "fossil_fuel": "Fossil fuel",
    "electrical_resistance": "Electric resistance",
    "heat_pump": "Heat pump",
}
BAT_FACET_ORDER = ["Electric resistance", "Heat pump", "Fossil fuel", "Other"]
BAT_BIN_WIDTH = 50
BAT_COLORS = {"overpaying": "#b71c1c", "underpaying": "#1b5e20"}


def _weighted_mean(df: pl.DataFrame, col: str, w_col: str) -> float:
    """Weighted arithmetic mean."""
    return float((df[col] * df[w_col]).sum() / df[w_col].sum())


def plot_bill_change_histogram(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    bin_width: int = BIN_WIDTH,
    title: str = "Change in Total Energy Bills after Switching to Heat Pumps",
) -> ggplot:
    """Weighted histogram of annual bill change."""
    snap = max(bin_width, 50)
    x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(df, "delta", "weight", 0.01) / snap) * snap
    x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(df, "delta", "weight", 0.99) / snap) * snap

    binned = (
        df.with_columns(
            ((pl.col("delta") / bin_width).floor() * bin_width + bin_width / 2).alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") <= -1000).then(pl.lit("savings > $1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") > -1000) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 0)).then(pl.lit("savings $0-1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") >= 0) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 1000)).then(pl.lit("losses $0-1k"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("losses > $1k"))
            .alias("quadrant"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "quadrant")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
    )

    pct = quadrant_pcts(df)
    quadrant_dtype = pl.Enum(QUADRANT_ORDER)
    plot_df = binned.with_columns(pl.col("quadrant").cast(quadrant_dtype))
    max_weight = cast(float, plot_df["weight_sum"].max()) if not plot_df.is_empty() else 0.0
    y_annot = max_weight * 1.05

    quadrant_bounds = [(x_lo, -1000), (-1000, 0), (0, 1000), (1000, x_hi)]
    label_xs = [(max(lo, x_lo) + min(hi, x_hi)) / 2 for lo, hi in quadrant_bounds]

    annotations = []
    for label, x_pos in zip(QUADRANT_ORDER, label_xs):
        if pct[label] < 0.05:
            continue
        annotations.append(
            annotate(
                "text", x=x_pos, y=y_annot,
                label=f"{label}\n{pct[label]:.1f}%",
                ha="center", size=11, color=QUADRANT_COLORS[label],
            )
        )

    p = (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="quadrant"))
        + geom_col(width=bin_width * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=[-1000, 0, 1000], linetype="dotted", color="gray")
        + scale_fill_manual(values=QUADRANT_COLORS, breaks=QUADRANT_ORDER)
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(x_lo, x_hi))
        + labs(x="Annual Bill Change ($)", y="# of Homes", title=title)
        + guides(fill=False)
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(figure_size=(10.5, 4.5))
    )
    for a in annotations:
        p = p + a
    return p


def plot_bat_histogram(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    col: str = "BAT_percustomer_total",
    title: str = "BAT per-customer (total): HP households under current rates",
) -> ggplot:
    """Weighted histogram of BAT per-customer values."""
    x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(df, col, "weight", 0.01) / 500) * 500
    x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(df, col, "weight", 0.99) / 500) * 500

    binned = (
        df.with_columns(
            ((pl.col(col) / BAT_BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BAT_BIN_WIDTH + BAT_BIN_WIDTH / 2).alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") < 0).then(pl.lit("underpaying"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("overpaying"))
            .alias("sign"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "sign")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
    )

    sign_dtype = pl.Enum(["underpaying", "overpaying"])
    plot_df = binned.with_columns(pl.col("sign").cast(sign_dtype))

    total_weight = df["weight"].sum()
    pct_over = df.filter(pl.col(col) >= 0)["weight"].sum() / total_weight * 100
    pct_under = 100 - pct_over
    w_mean = _weighted_mean(df, col, "weight")

    max_weight = cast(float, plot_df["weight_sum"].max()) if not plot_df.is_empty() else 0.0
    y_annot = max_weight * 1.08

    p = (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="sign"))
        + geom_col(width=BAT_BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="solid", color="gray", size=0.8)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=w_mean, linetype="dashed", color="#333333", size=0.7)
        + annotate(
            "text", x=w_mean, y=y_annot,
            label=f"mean: ${w_mean:,.0f}",
            ha="left" if w_mean >= 0 else "right",
            size=9, color="#333333",
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=BAT_COLORS)
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(x_lo, x_hi))
        + labs(x="BAT per-customer ($/year)", y="# of Homes (weighted)", title=title)
        + guides(fill=False)
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(figure_size=(10.5, 4.5))
    )

    if pct_under >= 1:
        p = p + annotate(
            "text", x=(x_lo + min(0, x_hi)) / 2, y=y_annot,
            label=f"Underpaying\n{pct_under:.0f}%",
            ha="center", size=10, color="#1b5e20",
        )
    if pct_over >= 1:
        p = p + annotate(
            "text", x=(max(0, x_lo) + x_hi) / 2, y=y_annot,
            label=f"Overpaying\n{pct_over:.0f}%",
            ha="center", size=10, color="#b71c1c",
        )

    return p


def plot_bat_histogram_faceted(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    col: str = "BAT_percustomer_total",
    title: str = "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type",
) -> ggplot:
    """Weighted histogram of BAT per-customer, vertically faceted by heating type."""
    facet_dtype = pl.Enum(BAT_FACET_ORDER)
    labeled = df.with_columns(
        pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL)
        .replace_strict(HEATING_TYPE_LABELS, default=pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL))
        .fill_null("Other")
        .cast(facet_dtype)
        .alias("heating_label"),
    )

    x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(labeled, col, "weight", 0.01) / 500) * 500
    x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(labeled, col, "weight", 0.99) / 500) * 500

    binned = (
        labeled.with_columns(
            ((pl.col(col) / BAT_BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BAT_BIN_WIDTH + BAT_BIN_WIDTH / 2).alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") < 0).then(pl.lit("underpaying"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("overpaying"))
            .alias("sign"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "sign", "heating_label")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
    )

    sign_dtype = pl.Enum(["underpaying", "overpaying"])
    plot_df = binned.with_columns(pl.col("sign").cast(sign_dtype))

    stat_rows = []
    mean_rows = []
    for ht in [f for f in BAT_FACET_ORDER if f in labeled["heating_label"].unique().to_list()]:
        sub = labeled.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
        tw = sub["weight"].sum()
        pct_over = sub.filter(pl.col(col) >= 0)["weight"].sum() / tw * 100
        w_mean = _weighted_mean(sub, col, "weight")
        facet_bins = plot_df.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
        y_max = cast(float, facet_bins["weight_sum"].max()) if not facet_bins.is_empty() else 0.0
        stat_rows.append({
            "heating_label": ht,
            "x": x_hi,
            "y": y_max * 0.92,
            "label": f"mean: ${w_mean:,.0f}  ·  {pct_over:.0f}% overpaying",
        })
        mean_rows.append({"heating_label": ht, "mean_x": w_mean})

    stats_df = pl.DataFrame(stat_rows).with_columns(pl.col("heating_label").cast(facet_dtype))
    means_df = pl.DataFrame(mean_rows).with_columns(pl.col("heating_label").cast(facet_dtype))
    n_facets = labeled["heating_label"].n_unique()

    return (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="sign"))
        + geom_col(width=BAT_BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="solid", color="gray", size=0.8)
        + geom_vline(
            data=means_df, mapping=aes(xintercept="mean_x"),
            linetype="dashed", color="#333333", size=0.7,
        )
        + geom_text(
            stats_df, aes(x="x", y="y", label="label"),
            ha="right", size=8, color="#333333",
            inherit_aes=False,
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=BAT_COLORS)
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(x_lo, x_hi))
        + facet_wrap("heating_label", ncol=1, scales="free_y")
        + labs(x="BAT per-customer ($/year)", y="# of Homes (weighted)", title=title)
        + guides(fill=False)
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(figure_size=(10.5, 2.25 * (1 + n_facets)))
    )
```

We need a version of `delta_state` with all fuel-type flags so we can slice bill
change histograms by heating fuel.

```{python}
#| label: dq-compute-bill-deltas-enriched

delta_enriched = (
    bills_u0
    .select(
        BLDG_ID, "weight", "sb.electric_utility",
        pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_before"),
        "heats_with_natgas", "heats_with_oil", "heats_with_propane",
        "postprocess_group.has_hp", HEATING_TYPE_COL,
    )
    .join(
        bills_u2.select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after")),
        on=BLDG_ID,
    )
    .with_columns((pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta"))
)
```

### Statewide

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bill-change-non-hp-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — non-heat-pump households (Statewide)"

non_hp_state = delta_enriched.filter(~pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp")).select("delta", "weight")
plot_bill_change_histogram(non_hp_state)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bill-change-natgas-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — natural gas-heated households (Statewide)"

natgas_state = delta_enriched.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas")).select("delta", "weight")
plot_bill_change_histogram(natgas_state)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bill-change-oil-propane-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — oil/propane-heated households (Statewide)"

oil_propane_state = (
    delta_enriched
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(oil_propane_state)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bill-change-elec-resistance-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — electric resistance-heated households (Statewide)"

elec_resistance_state = (
    delta_enriched
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(elec_resistance_state)
```

### ConEd

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bill-change-natgas-coned
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — natural gas-heated households (ConEd)"

delta_coned = delta_enriched.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == "coned")
natgas_coned = delta_coned.filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas")).select("delta", "weight")
plot_bill_change_histogram(natgas_coned, title="Bill change — natural gas, ConEd")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bill-change-oil-propane-coned
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — oil/propane-heated households (ConEd)"

oil_propane_coned = (
    delta_coned
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(oil_propane_coned, title="Bill change — oil/propane, ConEd")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bill-change-elec-resistance-coned
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — electric resistance-heated households (ConEd)"

elec_resistance_coned = (
    delta_coned
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(elec_resistance_coned, title="Bill change — elec resistance, ConEd")
```

## BAT histograms (diagnostic)

Weighted histograms of the Bill Alignment Test (BAT) per-customer metric. Positive
values indicate overpayment relative to cost of service; negative values indicate
underpayment. These were removed from the main analysis notebook during streamlining.

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bat-histogram-state
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) — all households, Statewide"

plot_bat_histogram(bat, title="BAT per-customer (total) — Statewide")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bat-histogram-state-faceted
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — Statewide"

plot_bat_histogram_faceted(bat, title="BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — Statewide")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bat-histogram-coned
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) — all households, ConEd"

bat_coned = bat.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == "coned")
plot_bat_histogram(bat_coned, title="BAT per-customer (total) — ConEd")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bat-histogram-coned-faceted
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — ConEd"

plot_bat_histogram_faceted(bat_coned, title="BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — ConEd")
```

### BAT under HP seasonal rate

```{python}
#| label: dq-load-bat-56

PATH_BAT_56 = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_5+6/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"
bat_56_dq = pl.read_parquet(PATH_BAT_56, hive_partitioning=True)
print(f"BAT table (run 5+6): {bat_56_dq.height} rows")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bat-histogram-state-hprate
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) — all households, Statewide (HP seasonal rate)"

plot_bat_histogram(bat_56_dq, title="BAT per-customer (total) — Statewide (HP seasonal rate)")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bat-histogram-state-faceted-hprate
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — Statewide (HP seasonal rate)"

plot_bat_histogram_faceted(bat_56_dq, title="BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — Statewide (HP seasonal rate)")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bat-histogram-coned-hprate
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) — all households, ConEd (HP seasonal rate)"

bat_coned_56 = bat_56_dq.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == "coned")
plot_bat_histogram(bat_coned_56, title="BAT per-customer (total) — ConEd (HP seasonal rate)")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bat-histogram-coned-faceted-hprate
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — ConEd (HP seasonal rate)"

plot_bat_histogram_faceted(bat_coned_56, title="BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — ConEd (HP seasonal rate)")
```

## Non-HP customer bill impact of cross-subsidy removal

If the cross-subsidy were removed — delivery rates realigned so HP customers no
longer overpay — what happens to non-HP customers' bills? Because HP customers
are still a small share of the population, the per-household impact is
negligible.

```{python}
#| label: dq-compute-delta-rebal-state

PATH_MASTER_BILLS_56 = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_5+6/comb_bills_year_target/"
bills_hprate_current = pl.read_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_56).filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)

delta_rebal_state = (
    bills_u0
    .select(BLDG_ID, "weight", pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_before"), "postprocess_group.has_hp")
    .join(
        bills_hprate_current.select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after")),
        on=BLDG_ID,
    )
    .with_columns((pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta"))
)
print(f"Rebalancing delta: {delta_rebal_state.height} rows")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dq-bill-change-non-hp-rebal-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change — all non-HP homes after cross-subsidy removal (Statewide)"

non_hp_rebal_state = (
    delta_rebal_state
    .filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == False)
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(
    non_hp_rebal_state, bin_width=5,
    title="Change in Non-HP Customer Bills after Cross-Subsidy Removal",
)
```
